task_completion.description: Verify the VeraCrypt volume is being created at the correct path with the correct size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the VeraCrypt Volume Creation Wizard showing the volume location set to '/home/ga/Volumes/legacy_driver_backup.iso' and the volume size set to 50 MB.

task_completion.description: Verify the VeraCrypt volume encryption, hash, filesystem, and password settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the VeraCrypt wizard screens showing Encryption Algorithm 'AES', Hash Algorithm 'SHA-512', Filesystem 'FAT', and the password field being filled (ideally with 'Display password' checked showing 'Camouflage2024!').

task_completion.description: Verify the newly created volume is mounted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the VeraCrypt main window showing '/home/ga/Volumes/legacy_driver_backup.iso' successfully mounted to a drive slot.

task_completion.description: Verify the exploit script is copied into the mounted volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window or terminal showing 'exploit_poc.py' located inside the mounted VeraCrypt volume directory (e.g., /media/veracrypt1/ or similar mount point).

task_completion.description: Verify the volume is dismounted after copying the file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the VeraCrypt main window showing that the previously used drive slot is now empty or a notification/terminal command indicating the volume was dismounted.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Last Modified' timestamp of the container file is changed to June 15, 2021 10:00.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window showing a 'touch' command modifying the timestamp of '/home/ga/Volumes/legacy_driver_backup.iso' (e.g., 'touch -m -t 202106151000' or similar), or an 'ls -l' / 'stat' output confirming the date is June 15, 2021 10:00.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the VeraCrypt application to create the volume.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine interaction with the VeraCrypt GUI windows (Volume Creation Wizard, Main Window) rather than just renaming an empty file to .iso.

integrity.description: Ensure terminal commands for copying or timestamp modification were actually executed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the presence of a new command prompt line after commands like 'cp' or 'touch' are typed, indicating the command was submitted and executed, not just typed into a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the timestamp modification is genuine and not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the timestamp change is performed via actual system commands (like 'touch') or file properties dialogs, rather than typing fake 'ls' output into a text editor.
